name: Bank Of 1889
description_short: Bank of 1889 is an FDIC-insured community bank based in Berryville, AR, offering deposit products, loans, credit cards, and digital banking services to personal and business customers.
description_long:
Bank of 1889 is a traditional community bank headquartered in Berryville, Arkansas, with a history tied to the bank's founding year of 1889. The institution operates as a full-service bank backed by FDIC insurance, meaning customer deposits up to $250,000 are protected by the federal government's deposit guarantee program. The bank maintains an employee-owner program, suggesting local ownership and community investment aligned with stakeholder interests rather than purely shareholder-driven models.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products spanning deposit accounts, lending, and ancillary services. For deposits, they provide personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and individual retirement accounts (IRAs). On the lending side, they originate real estate loans (residential and commercial), consumer loans, and commercial loans. Additional services include credit cards, trust services, safe deposit boxes, debit cards, and an extensive digital banking platform with mobile banking, online banking, bill pay, eStatements, mobile check capture, and Zelle integration for peer-to-peer payments.

Bank of 1889 distinguishes itself through its community bank model and local ownership structure via the employee-owner program. They actively engage in community sponsorship programs and operate bank-owned property auctions for vehicles and real estate. Their digital offerings are modernized through partnerships (Fiserv Solutions provides their mobile app available on Google Play), indicating investment in contemporary banking technology despite their historical foundation. The bank also provides educational resources through an education center and maintains transparency via published statements of condition.

As a traditional community bank, Bank of 1889 serves customers seeking personalized service and local decision-making, though they lack the brand recognition, extensive branch networks, or specialized products of larger institutions. Their website emphasizes stability and regulatory compliance but provides limited details on competitive rates, specific loan terms, credit card rewards, or pricing structures. Like most regional banks, they may have higher fees or less competitive rates than national banks or online-only competitors, and their service availability appears geographically concentrated in the Arkansas region. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile banking and mobile check capture
Online banking and business online banking
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Employee-owner program suggests local control and community investment alignment
Comprehensive deposit products including personal/business checking, savings, money market, CDs, and IRAs
Multiple lending options: real estate, consumer, and commercial loans
Modern digital banking platform with mobile app, online banking, bill pay, Zelle, and mobile check deposit
Trust services and safe deposit boxes for wealth and document management
Active community engagement through sponsorship programs and local ownership
Telebanking and eStatements options for convenient account management
cons: Limited online rate competitiveness information; no published APY/rate details on website
Geographically concentrated operations in Arkansas region limits accessibility for non-local customers
No details on credit card rewards, annual fees, or competitive advantages versus national card issuers
Website lacks specific loan term details, APR ranges, or qualification requirements
As a smaller regional bank, likely has fewer ATM locations and branch networks than national competitors
best_for: Arkansas and regional customers seeking FDIC-insured personal or business banking with local service
Small to mid-sized business owners needing commercial lending and business banking relationships
Community-minded customers preferring local, employee-owned institutions over national mega-banks
Customers seeking comprehensive banking services in one institution (deposits, loans, credit cards, trust services)
diagnosis: Bank of 1889 is best for regional customers in Arkansas seeking FDIC-insured deposit and lending products from a locally-owned, community-focused institution with modern digital banking capabilities. The main caveat is that as a smaller regional bank, they likely offer less competitive rates and fewer service locations than national banks, and detailed pricing information is not publicly available on their website, requiring direct inquiry for rate comparison.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
